name: Sweet-And-Sour Red Peppers
recipeIngredient: 1 tbsp Tomato paste (the kind you squeeze out of a tube)
Small basil leaves (left whole) or Large Basil leaves (shredded)
recipeInstructions:
1. Slice the peppers into strips 2- to 3-inches long and about 1/4-inch wide.
They can be very skinny, or nice and wide, too.
2. Heat oil in a large skillet with the garlic until hot.
3. Add the peppers, sprinkled with salt, and stir so that all are coated with the oil.
4. Cook over a brisk heat, shaking the pan occasionally until the peppers are hot and beginning to cook, about 5 minutes.
5. Stir in the tomato paste. When it is well incorporated, add the vinegar and stir.
6. Continue to saute until the vinegar is reduced and the peppers are as done as you like.
They're good when still somewhat firm—perfect for a little salad—but they can also cook until they are very soft throughout—good with pasta or heaped on garlic-rubbed grilled bread.
7. Toss in the basil, season with pepper, and heap onto a serving plate.
